mechanism_narrative: A high and rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating household financial health, leading to reduced consumer spending, lower corporate earnings expectations, and negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 8.0% and has risen over the last 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC labor market weakness persists in 2011
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: When policy rates are at the zero lower bound, an acceleration in consumer price inflation erodes real returns and signals a potential limit to further monetary accommodation, leading to a negative repricing of risk assets.
failure_condition: The correlation between CPIAUCSL 3-month change and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is positive during periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: The 6-month change in retail and food services sales is a direct indicator of consumer demand momentum. Positive momentum translates into higher expected corporate revenues and drives positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending constitutes the majority of US GDP
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 6-month change in RSAFS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates robust business activity and capacity utilization, leading to higher corporate profits and positive equity market performance over a medium-term horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is a core component of the Coincident Economic Activity Index
failure_condition: The 3-month change in INDPRO fails to show a positive and significant correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over the full sample period.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth is positive but housing starts are stagnant or falling, it signals a fragile, imbalanced economic recovery. This specific weakness in a key cyclical sector leads to negative revisions of future growth, hurting equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year GDPC1 change > 1% AND Year-over-year HOUST change < 0%
failure_condition: In periods matching the trigger condition, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A significant positive surprise in nonfarm payrolls signals an unexpected acceleration in economic activity, leading to immediate upward revisions in earnings forecasts and positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:NFP is a key high-frequency market moving economic release
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a PAYEMS release > 200k is not statistically different from the average return on other days.

mechanism_narrative: A falling trend in the effective federal funds rate signals continued or increasing monetary accommodation. This lowers the discount rate for future earnings and supports higher equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed is pursuing unconventional easing policies in 2011
failure_condition: The 3-month change in FEDFUNDS has a positive or zero correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts below a critical historical threshold indicates a severe contraction in construction and investment, acting as a leading indicator for a broader recession and causing a negative repricing of equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical role of housing downturns in preceding recessions
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 750k, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically significantly negative compared to periods when HOUST is above 750k.
